mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, with Rank IC consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points within a single month signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate equity sell-offs as investors price in falling consumer spending and corporate earnings.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes above the 0.5pp threshold, invalidating the sell-off mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and durable goods demand, driving sustained corporate revenue growth and positive equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both grow > 2% month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 over any 2-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 6-month moving average signals supply-side bottlenecks and manufacturing weakness, which suppresses equity valuations during late-cycle expansions when growth expectations are already priced in.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy falling below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate combined with stable CPIAUCSL signals accommodative monetary policy without inflationary pressure, generating immediate positive equity momentum over the following week.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 5-day returns in over 60% of observed instances, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent month-over-month declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening labor demand, which systematically reduces household income growth and corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines show no predictive power for 60-day forward returns, with a rolling Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 3.0% confirms robust aggregate demand, which forces equity analysts to upgrade earnings forecasts and drives sustained positive S&P 500 returns over the next month as capital flows into risk assets.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive following GDPC1 releases above 3.0%, with a hit rate below 50% over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production below 90.0 signals severe manufacturing contraction, which triggers immediate equity liquidation during stagflationary regimes as investors flee cyclical stocks amid rising input costs.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings below 90.0 fail to predict negative 5-day returns during stagflationary periods, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 40%.
